title: Resinanthus acutatus
taxonomy: Resinanthus acutatus is classified as a genus of flowering plants in the family Icacinaceae, an order of flowering plants commonly known as the bugbane or icacinales. The species Resinanthus acutatus is native to Australia and is characterized by its distinctive foliage and woody stems. It is a member of the Icacinaceae family, which is a small, primarily tropical family of plants. The Icacinaceae family is closely related to the Apocynaceae and Boraginaceae families, and together they form a distinct clade within the order Gentianales. The classification of Resinanthus acutatus reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, providing potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and infections.
